english: I'm on my way.
situation: Use when you are heading somewhere and want to reassure the other person.

english: Could you speak a little slower?
situation: Useful when you need clearer listening support in a real conversation.

english: I need a few more minutes.
situation: Use when you are almost ready but need a short delay.

english: Let's grab coffee after work.
situation: A casual invitation for an after-work meetup.

english: I forgot to bring my charger.
situation: Useful when something important is missing.

english: What time does the train leave?
situation: Good for asking schedules in stations or terminals.

english: I have already finished my homework.
situation: A simple statement about completed work.

english: This idea sounds interesting.
situation: Use when you want to respond positively to a suggestion.

english: Could I ask you one quick question?
situation: Polite and natural for opening a short question.

english: I think we should wait a little longer.
situation: A gentle way to suggest patience.

english: I am looking for a quieter place to work.
korean: 조용하게 일할 수 있는 곳을 찾고 있어.
situation: Useful for describing your environment preference.

english: The meeting has been moved to tomorrow morning.
situation: A practical update about schedule changes.

english: I was wondering if you could help me with this.
korean: 이거 좀 도와주실 수 있는지 궁금했어요.
situation: A polite and natural request for help.

english: She explained the problem very clearly.
situation: Useful when describing how well someone communicated.

english: I will send you the updated file by noon.
situation: A common workplace promise with a deadline.

english: It took longer than I expected.
situation: A natural way to express an unexpected delay.

english: We should probably make a backup first.
situation: Practical advice before changing important files.

english: The restaurant was surprisingly crowded.
situation: Use to describe an unexpectedly busy place.

english: I am not sure whether this is the best option.
situation: Useful when you want to express hesitation.

english: Could you recommend a good place nearby?
korean: 근처에 괜찮은 곳 추천해 주실 수 있나요?
situation: Great for asking for local recommendations.

english: I have been practicing English every morning.
korean: 나는 매일 아침 영어를 연습하고 있어.
situation: A present perfect continuous sentence about habit.

english: The instructions were easier than I thought.
situation: Useful when reflecting on learning materials.

english: I am trying to stay organized this week.
korean: 이번 주에는 정리 정돈을 잘하려고 노력 중이야.
situation: A natural sentence about personal routine.

english: Please let me know if anything changes.
situation: A polite request for updates.

english: The weather suddenly got much colder.
situation: Good for describing a quick weather change.

english: I would like to try something new today.
korean: 오늘은 뭔가 새로운 걸 해보고 싶어.
situation: Useful for making a positive plan.

english: Can we move this conversation to later?
korean: 이 대화를 나중으로 미룰 수 있을까요?
situation: Polite when you need to pause a talk.

english: I finally found the document I was looking for.
situation: A satisfying update about finding something.

english: The app keeps crashing whenever I open it.
korean: 그 앱은 열기만 하면 계속 멈춰.
situation: A realistic sentence for reporting a bug.

english: I am happy to help if you need anything.
situation: Warm and friendly for offering support.

english: The guide covered the main points very well.
korean: 그 안내서는 핵심 내용을 아주 잘 다뤘어.
situation: Useful when evaluating learning material.

english: We arrived earlier than planned.
situation: A simple travel update.

english: He gave me a very practical suggestion.
situation: Useful when you want to praise advice.

english: I need to double-check the numbers before I answer.
korean: 답하기 전에 숫자를 다시 확인해야 해.
situation: A careful response when accuracy matters.

english: The line was longer than I expected.
situation: Useful for describing waiting situations.

english: I am interested in learning how this works.
situation: Good for asking about a system or device.

english: The conversation became more relaxed after dinner.
korean: 저녁 식사 후에 대화가 더 편해졌어.
situation: Useful for describing a mood change.

english: She was confident even under pressure.
situation: Useful when describing someone's attitude.

english: I left my umbrella at home again.
korean: 나 또 우산을 집에 두고 왔어.
situation: A casual sentence about a small mistake.

english: The report needs one more careful edit.
korean: 보고서는 한 번만 더 꼼꼼하게 수정하면 돼.
situation: Great for speaking about revision.

english: I did not notice the difference at first.
situation: Useful when comparing two things.

english: Let's meet somewhere with a nice view.
situation: A friendly suggestion for a meetup location.

english: I was surprised by how fast it happened.
korean: 그 일이 얼마나 빨리 일어났는지 놀랐어.
situation: Useful for expressing surprise.

english: The package should arrive sometime this afternoon.
situation: Useful when tracking a delivery.

english: I would rather stay home and rest tonight.
korean: 오늘 밤은 집에서 쉬는 게 더 좋아.
situation: Natural way to express preference.

english: Could you send me a screenshot of the issue?
korean: 문제 화면 캡처를 보내주실 수 있나요?
situation: Very useful for technical support.

english: I feel more focused when the room is quiet.
korean: 방이 조용하면 더 집중이 잘 돼.
situation: Use to describe your ideal study environment.

english: The conversation drifted away from the original topic.
situation: Useful when a discussion goes off track.

english: I need to make sure I understand the details correctly.
korean: 세부 사항을 정확히 이해했는지 확인해야 해.
situation: Useful for careful communication.

english: The solution was simpler than everyone expected.
korean: 해결책은 모두가 예상한 것보다 더 단순했어.
situation: Good for describing an unexpected outcome.

english: After a short walk, the noise started to fade.
korean: 잠깐 걸으니 소음이 서서히 줄어들기 시작했어.
situation: Useful for describing gradual change.

english: He handled the situation with calm confidence.
situation: A polished phrase for describing behavior.

english: I am considering whether to apply for the position.
situation: Useful for discussing an important decision.

english: The museum was packed with visitors all afternoon.
korean: 그 박물관은 오후 내내 방문객들로 가득했어.
situation: Good for describing a busy place in a travel context.

english: I would appreciate a quick update when you have time.
korean: 시간 되실 때 간단히 상황 알려주시면 감사하겠습니다.
situation: Polite and professional in messages.

english: The article raised more questions than it answered.
korean: 그 글은 답보다 질문을 더 많이 남겼어.
situation: A useful pattern for critical reading.

english: It might be better to leave this for tomorrow.
korean: 이건 내일로 미루는 게 더 나을지도 몰라.
situation: A soft and cautious suggestion.

english: The team reached a compromise after a long discussion.
korean: 팀은 긴 논의 끝에 타협점을 찾았다.
situation: Useful in business or group decision-making.

english: I am trying to build a habit that actually lasts.
korean: 나는 진짜로 오래가는 습관을 만들려고 노력 중이야.
situation: Great for talking about personal growth.

english: This is the kind of practice that pays off over time.
korean: 이런 연습은 시간이 지나면 분명 효과가 있어.
situation: A motivating sentence for learning consistency.
